title: #sales: 1769014420
preview:
Hannah Schmitt: Need quick help on EU data residency objection. Prospect (EU bank) wants “EU-only processing + EU-only storage + no US access” for Hosted. What’s the approved phrasing + what do we *not* promise? @Olga Petrov @Daniel Carter @Marcus Lin

Lukas Meyer: +1, similar objection came up in EMEA last week. They’re asking if *anything* touches US systems (logs, support tooling, etc). Want to keep this clean.

Avery Johnson: Same here on another deal. Can we get a pasteable paragraph + a “don’t say this” list?

Priyom Das: They also asked if model routing could ever fail over to a non-EU region under load. Need to confirm.

Marcus Lin: jumping in. First: do NOT promise “EU-only” unless we’re talking Private deployment w/ customer-controlled infra, or we have a written contract amendment that explicitly defines what’s in/out.

Marcus Lin: For Hosted: we can support region selection (incl EU) for request processing, but we should be precise that some operational metadata may be processed/stored outside the selected region depending on the system (billing, abuse prevention, support). We need to use the approved language.

Naomi Feldman: +1. Also: don’t imply “no failover ever.” Our default is to keep traffic in-region if you’ve selected EU, but we can’t blanket guarantee “never leaves EU under any condition” unless it’s explicitly in the commercial terms / Private. Say “region-pinned” and “no cross-region routing by default for customer content” (but avoid absolute words like “never”).

Hannah Schmitt: got it. What’s the exact approved snippet? They’re literally asking for “contractual commitment that all data incl logs stays in EU and only EU staff can access.”

Olga Petrov: Here’s what we can say (approved style). Use “Customer Content” vs “data” to avoid ambiguity. Also do not commit on “EU-only personnel” access.

Olga Petrov: Pasteable response (Hosted + EU region):
“Redwood Inference supports regional processing for Hosted API workloads. When a customer selects an EU region, Customer Content (request prompts and model outputs) is processed in that region. Access to Customer Content is restricted to authorized personnel on a need-to-know basis and is logged. Certain limited operational metadata (e.g., account and billing information, service health telemetry) may be processed or stored outside the selected region as part of operating and securing the service. For stricter residency requirements (including customer-managed networking and data boundary controls), Redwood offers Dedicated capacity in-region and Private (VPC/on‑prem) deployments.”

Daniel Carter: + a compliance note: we should add “Details available under NDA / via the evidence request process.” Do not attach internal diagrams in email.

Daniel Carter: Also, for “contractual commitment” requests: route to Legal + GRC. We can say “We can evaluate specific contractual residency requirements; please share the questionnaire language.”

Lukas Meyer: Can we explicitly address the “failover” question? They’re worried about cross-region fallback.

Marcus Lin: approved approach: “We route based on the region selected; cross-region routing of customer content is not the standard behavior for region-pinned setups.” Don’t use “never.” If they require a hard guarantee, push to Dedicated/Private + contract language.

Naomi Feldman: Also don’t conflate “data residency” with “data retention.” They’ll ask “how long do you store prompts/logs in EU.” That’s a separate set of knobs depending on deployment.

Avery Johnson: Can you list the top “DON’T SAY” bullets? Helps AEs avoid stepping on landmines.

Avery Johnson: seeing the same security Qs pop up again this week. can we get a canonical list + where in the FAQ to point? dumping what I got from 3 prospects:
- “Do you train/fine-tune on our prompts or outputs?”
- data retention: “how long do you keep prompts/logs? can we set to 0?”
- EU residency: “can you guarantee all data stays in EU? what about support access?”
- SOC 2: “can we have the report? under NDA?”
- ISO: “are you ISO27001 certified?”
- audit logs: “what events are logged? can we export to SIEM?”
- SSO/SCIM: “okta/azure ad? does SCIM cover deprovisioning?”
- pen test: “frequency + can we see summary?”

Priyom Das: +1. Summit is asking super specifically:
- do we store full prompts in logs by default?
- can they disable logging for prod traffic?
- do we have separate retention for app logs vs audit logs?
- who are subprocessors + where’s the canonical list?

Hannah Schmitt: Nordbank angle:
- “data residency” vs “data processing” — they want wording they can paste into their questionnaire
- “cross-border transfer” + if any US-based engineers can access EU prod
- incident notification window (“within X hours?”)

Stephanie Nguyen: thanks. yes, we need to consolidate and point to the refreshed Enterprise Security FAQ + owners per section. can folks add any other repeated Qs here? also pls note which deployment mode (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private) the prospect is evaluating.

Avery Johnson: Hosted + Dedicated (they’re starting Hosted, want path to Dedicated). also asked:
- “Is customer data used to improve models?” (they equate to training)
- “Can we bring our own keys / KMS?”

Priyom Das: Hosted only right now, but they mentioned future VPC. also asked about:
- encryption in transit / at rest (TLS versions etc)
- whether we support HSM

Jordan Blake: looping in Security/GRC/SE. we need to answer quickly but carefully (no roadmap promises). Naomi/Olga/Marcus/Markus — can we get canonical snippets + where they live? right now reps are copy/pasting from old docs.

Markus Klein: from SE side, a lot of confusion is coming from “what’s true in Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private.” we should standardize answers with a prefix like:
- Hosted: Redwood-managed infra, standard controls
- Dedicated: isolated capacity + stronger data isolation story
- Private: customer-managed networking/keys options (depending)

Fatima Noor: regulated accounts keep asking whether Private means:
- single-tenant control plane vs shared control plane
- if we support on-prem / air-gapped
we need exact language + the “what we do/don’t say” guardrails.

Naomi Feldman: +1. also audit logging questions are all over the place. we should not say “full SIEM integration everywhere” unless we’re explicit about what’s available today. can we align on the exact event taxonomy + export destinations we support now.

Olga Petrov: for SOC 2 + evidence: we can’t attach the report in Slack. canonical process is: NDA -> evidence request -> GRC sends from the restricted folder. please point customers to the evidence request playbook and do not forward artifacts.

Marcus Lin: on “training on customer data” — we need consistent definition. approved phrasing should clarify:
- we do not use customer prompts/outputs to train Redwood-provided models by default
- any optional customer-requested fine-tuning/training would be explicitly contracted and isolated
pls don’t improvise “never ever” language without the qualifier.

Camila Reyes: can also help with tightening the wording for sales-safe copy (esp ISO + residency). we should keep it competitive-safe: explain our posture without making certification claims we can’t substantiate.

Dev Patel: support tickets echo these exact Qs. top two from the last month:
1) “do you train on my data?”
2) “can you delete everything + what’s the timeline?”
I can pull a few sanitized snippets for what prospects are asking + where the current FAQ is confusing.

Stephanie Nguyen: action plan:
1) AEs/SEs keep dumping Qs in this thread (with account + deployment mode)
2) I’ll consolidate into the intake doc + tag owners per section
3) Security/GRC provide approved snippets + we publish the refreshed FAQ as the single source of truth

Avery Johnson: adding more (Acme Health):
- HIPAA/BAA: “will you sign a BAA?” (we should have a standard stance)
- “where are your audit logs stored and for how long?”
- “do you have SSO for the console AND API access?”

Markus Klein: re SSO: we should distinguish:
- console login via SAML SSO
- API auth is via API keys/service creds (and governance via RBAC)
- SCIM is for provisioning in the org directory
people conflate all of these.

Olga Petrov: on ISO27001: approved language is “alignment” unless/until certified. do not say “ISO certified” unless Legal/GRC confirms current status. we can map controls to Annex A themes in a one-pager.

Hannah Schmitt: for EU residency, can we get a 2-3 sentence block that covers:
- where request processing happens
- where logs/telemetry go
- support access boundaries
They keep asking “guarantee EU-only” and it’s easy to overcommit.

Marcus Lin: residency block needs caveats:
- region availability depends on deployment mode
- some metadata may be processed outside region for operations (if true) — we need to be precise
- for Private/VPC we can support stricter network boundaries
Let’s only use approved copy from the kit.

Naomi Feldman: also add a standard answer for incident response:
- how we notify customers
- typical timeframes are contractual (don’t promise in Slack)
- where to find the security addendum language

Jordan Blake: can we please include “where does this live” in the final answer. reps need a link, not a paragraph in Slack.

Stephanie Nguyen: yup. once refreshed, we’ll link everything from the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit index page in Confluence. interim:
- if you need SOC 2 report: use the evidence request process (NDA required)
- if questionnaire asks for something we don’t support: use the exceptions policy + escalate

Dev Patel: i’ll DM Stephanie a short list of the most common phrasing customers use (so the FAQ headings match what they search for).

Camila Reyes: when we have the draft snippets, I can do a quick pass to ensure consistency across FAQ + one-pagers + RFP library. biggest risk right now is 3 different versions of the same answer.

Priyom Das: request: can we get a copy/paste block for “audit log export” with what’s supported (S3/SIEM/etc) and what fields are included? lots of security questionnaires want exact fields.

Naomi Feldman: yes, but we’ll validate with Security Eng first. I’ll coordinate with Marcus to confirm current behavior vs docs.

Stephanie Nguyen: cool. keep adding Qs here through EOD tomorrow. I’ll publish a consolidated list + owners (Security/GRC/SE/PMM) and a temporary snippet doc until the FAQ refresh ships.

Avery Johnson: :thumbsup: will add if anything else comes in. biggest ask from me is the canonical “no training on customer data” paragraph + link so we stop rewriting it.

Marcus Lin: acknowledged. we’ll provide the approved paragraph and the “what we do/don’t say” notes so folks don’t accidentally drift.

Jordan Blake: thanks all. once we have the canonical snippets, we’ll also add them to HubSpot snippets / templates so it’s one click for reps.

title: #sales: 1765439999
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up: FinEdge wants an EU-only POC w/ BYO KMS and APAC only as a disaster recovery target (no active routing). They also want a clear list of subprocessors and an egress diagram for legal. Can presales assemble a quick response + ballpark price delta by EOD?

Luca Romano: Quick Q: Which BYO KMS flavors? They mentioned 'customer-managed keys' but didn't specify provider. We can support AWS KMS in hosted via envelope encryption; Dedicated/Private supports direct customer KMS calls (AWS/GCP/Azure) or HSMs. Need to confirm.

Priya Singh: For hosted with BYO the model is: customer wraps a DEK and uploads, we store wrapped key + attest. If they require direct KMS API calls from compute nodes, that's Private/Dedicated only. I'll add this to the reply.

Marta Kovac: If they absolutely forbid any non-EU subprocessors, note that our control plane currently touches a US analytics pipeline for hosted telemetry. We can offer Dedicated with EU-only subprocessors but it adds provisioning time and cost.

Luca Romano: Cost note: Tenant pinning + reserved capacity in eu-west-1 typically adds ~20-30% to the hosted run-rate for small POCs; BYO-KMS ops + attestation adds a fixed onboarding fee (one-time) ~= $1k-$3k depending on risk review. I'll scope more precise numbers if we have expected qps.

Ava Chen: Can someone draft an ops-friendly routing policy snippet and a short list of subprocessors we can share? Legal will want specifics. Also need to confirm whether APAC cold DR will ever touch control plane.

infra-bot: Automated note: current subprocessor inventory (prod) includes: aws-eu-west-1, aws-eu-central-1, redwood-monitoring-eu, redwood-cdn-global. Hosted telemetry pipeline may use us-east-1 for aggregated analytics unless opt-out is configured. See /ops/subprocessors for full list.

Priya Singh: Draft routing policy (example):
```
tenant: finedge-poc
pin_region: eu-west-1
compute_failover: apac-cold (standby, no state replication)
subprocessors_allowed: [aws-eu-west-1, aws-eu-central-1, redwood-cdn-eu]
control_plane: eu-only (if Dedicated requested)
```

Marta Kovac: If they accept our hosted telemetry opt-out and sign a restricted subproc addendum we can avoid US telemetry. If they don't, Dedicated/Private is the only compliant path. Provisioning lead time for Dedicated EU-only = 6-10 weeks.

Luca Romano: Action items: Priya -> BYO KMS hosting vs Private matrix + wrapping explanation; Marta -> official subprocessor list + opt-out wording; Ava -> share ballpark numbers to customer (include reserve capacity delta and one-time onboarding fee).

Ava Chen: I'll ping FinEdge with the high-level ballpark and ask for expected tokens/day and desired RPS to refine costs. Will include timeline note about Dedicated provisioning if they insist on zero non-EU subprocessors. Thanks all :rocket:

preview:
Maya Ortiz: Heads-up: BankWave POC asked for EU-only data residency, BYO-KMS (they prefer Azure Key Vault), and APAC only as cold DR. Need presales summary + quick ballpark delta by tomorrow.

Ben Carter: Quick clarification: Azure Key Vault direct calls from our hosted compute are not supported. Hosted supports envelope encryption (customer wraps DEK). Dedicated/Private can do direct Azure/GCP/AWS calls.

Sofia Alvarez: For legal they want a named subprocessor list and egress diagram showing control plane vs compute. We should make clear whether our telemetry touches non-EU regions unless they opt-out.

infra-bot: Subprocessor snapshot: aws-eu-west-1, aws-eu-central-1, redwood-monitoring-eu, redwood-cdn-global. Hosted telemetry may route aggregated metrics via us-east-1 unless opt-out is enabled.

Ben Carter: Cost note: tenant pinning to eu-west-1 + reserved capacity for a small POC typically +20-30% on hosted run-rate. BYO-KMS onboarding fee one-time ~$1k. Dedicated EU-only provisioning lead time 6-8 weeks.

Maya Ortiz: Can someone draft a one-paragraph summary and a routing policy snippet I can forward? Keep it high-level for the customer.

Sofia Alvarez: Draft summary: "We can run your POC with EU-only compute. For BYO Key Vault, hosted mode requires envelope-wrapped keys; direct KMS calls require Dedicated/Private. APAC can be configured as cold DR only (standby) but fully EU-only subprocessors requires Dedicated and ~6-8 week provisioning. Estimated pricing delta for POC: +20-30% run-rate and ~$1k onboarding."

Ben Carter: Routing policy snippet:
```
tenant: bankwave-poc
pin_region: eu-west-1

Egress & private connectivity,Internet / peering,Private peering options,Direct network,Quoted separately,true

title: #sales: 1750504321
preview:
Olivia Park: Heads-up — large enterprise prospect wants definitive breach notification timelines and will only accept a flat 72-hour contractual clause. Also asked for CAIQ evidence and RCA timing.

Ben Carter: We should avoid an unconditional 72h promise. Operationally we target initial customer notification within 72 hours of confirmed incident when practicable. Important to distinguish 'detection' vs 'confirmation'.

Mei Wong: Agreed. Suggest wording: "Upon confirmation of a Security Incident materially affecting Customer Data, Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to provide Customer with an initial notification within seventy-two (72) hours of confirmation where practicable. Notification timing may be delayed for law enforcement or forensic actions; Redwood will notify Customer if such constraints apply."

Legal Bot: +1. Add required content: severity, affected systems, preliminary impact, mitigation steps, and named point of contact in the initial notice.

Olivia Park: They also want evidence (CAIQ) and proof of drills/runbooks. What can we share under NDA?

Ben Carter: We can provide a redacted CAIQ snapshot and SOC 2 summary under NDA. For IR proof, offer a redacted runbook excerpt and our tabletop cadence: annual company-wide + quarterly service drills for Dedicated customers.

Mei Wong: Runbook excerpt (redacted):

"Triage (0-4h): validate alert, assess scope, classify severity
Containment (4-24h): isolate affected hosts/services, apply mitigations
Initial notification (24-72h): prepare customer notice, legal review, escalate to on-call
Remediation (72h+): apply fixes, validate remediation, begin RCA
Post-incident (30-90d): draft RCA, final report and follow-up actions"

Olivia Park: RCA timing?

IR Oncall: Preliminary findings usually within 7-14 business days; draft RCA ~30 days; final RCA 30-90 days depending on third-party forensics.

Ben Carter: Also clarify channels: primary = customer's designated security contact (email + phone), account lead; secondary = customer portal advisory for Dedicated/Private. We do not independently file regulator notices on behalf of customers unless explicitly authorized in contract. We provide artifacts and reasonable assistance for customer filings.

Olivia Park: Prospect asked if we publish incident history.

Mei Wong: We keep internal incident logs and provide customer-specific post-incident reports to affected customers. Public advisories/postmortems are published only for broad platform incidents on status.redwood.com.

Olivia Park: I'll draft the response with the proposed clause, redacted CAIQ offer (NDA), runbook excerpt, RCA cadence, and Dedicated SLA options (24x7 on-call + 1-hour Sev1 ack). Please review before I send.

Ben Carter: I'll review in 30 mins.

Legal Bot: Contract templates: https://internal.redwood/contracts/ir-clauses (access: legal-sales).

Olivia Park: Thanks everyone :thumbsup:

title: #sales: 1749281162
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: hey team — for outbound sequences using the benchmark report, what CTA do we want AEs/SDRs to push for enterprise prospects?

Stephanie Nguyen: seeing reps mix “start trial” vs “book demo” vs “Dedicated sizing” and it’s getting inconsistent

Grace O'Connor: +1. SDR side: if we push “trial” to a security/procurement-heavy account we lose them. but if we push “demo” too early for devtools-y prospects we lose them too.

Markus Klein: from SE perspective, for true enterprise (VPC/Private/Dedicated) the best CTA is “15-min sizing + workload fit consult” (not generic demo). gets us to: region, concurrency, ctx length, p95 SLO, data residency.

Markus Klein: also recommend we *always* include the methodology link so it doesn’t feel like marketing fluff. prospects ask “what was the workload?” within 1 reply.

Lukas Meyer: EMEA angle: CTA should be “talk to us about EU region + data residency options” (Dedicated in eu-west / Private VPC). If we push “trial” they ask about DPAs + procurement before even clicking.

Soojin Lee: we aligned in PMM/legal review that we should avoid “we’re fastest” language. Use “see results for your region + model, and we’ll help you map to your workload.”

Soojin Lee: CTA guidance draft:
- Enterprise: “Book a 20‑min benchmark walkthrough + sizing”
- Mid-market/PLG: “Try the interactive table + start with $ credits”
- Regulated: “Talk to us about Private/VPC + audit logs”

Ben Carter: from growth/ops: we can support 2 primary CTAs on the landing page without messing attribution:
1) “Explore interactive results” (top funnel)
2) “Talk to an expert” (demo request)
Ben Carter: for sales outbound, I’d default to #2 for enterprise, and use #1 as the proof point link in the body.

Ethan Wallace: + we have a short “how we measured” explainer section we can link (not the full methodology doc) for colder outbound. Full methodology for when they reply with questions.

Jasmine Liu: CS/support note: we’re already getting tickets asking “did you enable caching/batching?” — in outbound we should preempt w/ one line: “numbers reflect Redwood-recommended production settings; details in methodology.”

Stephanie Nguyen: ok so for enterprise sequences: proof point link = interactive table or PDF; CTA = benchmark walkthrough + sizing call.

Stephanie Nguyen: anyone have suggested email copy we can hand to AEs/SDRs?

Markus Klein: here’s a template we’ve used in live calls (feel free to paste into sequences). keep it tight.

Markus Klein: 
Subject: Latency/$ benchmarks for {region} + {model} (incl. methodology)

Hi {Name} — we just published a production-focused benchmark report comparing latency/$ across models and regions.

Two reasons it might be relevant for {Company}:
- We break out p50/p95 + $/1M tokens (not just tokens/sec)
- Results are consistent across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments (same runtime)

Link: {interactive table} (methodology linked in-page)

If helpful, happy to do a 20‑min walkthrough and map the results to your workload (context length, concurrency, and p95 SLO). Worth a quick sizing call next week?

— {Sender}

(reaction: :thumbsup: from Markus Klein)

Grace O'Connor: SDR version w/ clearer ask:

“Open to a quick 15 min to sanity-check which model+region hits your p95 and cost target? If yes, who’s best to pull in from eng?”

Grace O'Connor: and if they click but don’t reply, step 2 email can be: “want me to send the exact config/run manifest we used for {region}?” (only if we’re allowed to share; otherwise “methodology details”).

Soojin Lee: careful on “run manifest” language — we can share methodology + what’s published, but don’t promise raw logs. Legal wants us to avoid implying we’ll hand over internal artifacts broadly.

Ethan Wallace: +1. Phrase as “configuration summary (model version, region, token mix, concurrency)” and point them to the methodology appendix.

Lukas Meyer: also for EMEA add 1 line: “We can support EU data residency via Dedicated capacity in-region or Private VPC deployments.” Otherwise they assume US-only.

Ben Carter: for sequences, please keep UTM consistent (use the sales enablement link shortener). we want attribution to the benchmark campaign in HubSpot.

Stephanie Nguyen: got it. action items:
- I’ll post a short CTA rule-of-thumb for AEs/SDRs (enterprise = walkthrough+sizing)
- Markus/Grace: can you drop the two email variants in the enablement folder?

Markus Klein: yep will add today.

Grace O'Connor: on it.

Soojin Lee: I’ll update the talk track doc w/ the CTA guidance + safe language reminder.

Stephanie Nguyen: thank you all. :pray:

Grace O'Connor: and if they click but don’t reply, step 2 email can be: “want me to send the exact config/run manifest we used for {region}?” (only if we’re allowed to share; otherwise “methodology details”).

Soojin Lee: careful on “run manifest” language — we can share methodology + what’s published, but don’t promise raw logs. Legal wants us to avoid implying we’ll hand over internal artifacts broadly.

Ethan Wallace: +1. Phrase as “configuration summary (model version, region, token mix, concurrency)” and point them to the methodology appendix.

Lukas Meyer: also for EMEA add 1 line: “We can support EU data residency via Dedicated capacity in-region or Private VPC deployments.” Otherwise they assume US-only.

Ben Carter: for sequences, please keep UTM consistent (use the sales enablement link shortener). we want attribution to the benchmark campaign in HubSpot.

Stephanie Nguyen: got it. action items:
- I’ll post a short CTA rule-of-thumb for AEs/SDRs (enterprise = walkthrough+sizing)
- Markus/Grace: can you drop the two email variants in the enablement folder?

Markus Klein: yep will add today.

Grace O'Connor: on it.

Soojin Lee: I’ll update the talk track doc w/ the CTA guidance + safe language reminder.

title: #sales: 1747506100
preview:
Isabelle Laurent: quick q for the pilot — we need consistent outcome logging in HubSpot so we can attribute back to the PLG signals. Can we align on *what* AEs/SDRs mark + where? I’m seeing a mix of notes + deal stages + random task comments.

Grace O'Connor: +1. SDRs need a simple dropdown, otherwise it won’t happen. What are the required outcomes?

Ben Carter: for measurement we need at minimum: Contacted, Meeting booked, Eval/POC started, Opp created, Disqualified. Ideally also “No response”. We can roll up to conversion funnel.

Avery Johnson: what counts as “contacted”? if I send email #1 in the cadence, that’s contacted? or only if they reply?

Isabelle Laurent: good call. I’d define:
- Attempted (outreach sent, no reply yet)
- Contacted (two-way interaction: reply, call connect, or Slack/intro)
But if we’re adding too many statuses it gets messy.

Tyler Benson: from ops side: fewer statuses is better. We can infer “attempted” from task completion timestamps if we standardize task templates.

David Kim: HubSpot mechanics:
1) tasks can have `Status` + `Completion notes` + `Task type`
2) we can add a custom *Company property* like `plg_signal_outcome_latest`
3) we can add a custom *Deal property* like `plg_signal_attribution_id` if/when a deal is created
If we want clean reporting, a property > free-text.

Stephanie Nguyen: proposal: keep it simple for AEs.
Required logging when you close the “PLG Signal Follow-up” task:
- Outcome (dropdown)
- If disqualified: reason (dropdown)
Everything else optional.

Grace O'Connor: +1. Make it on the task, not “remember to update company record”. SDRs live in tasks.

David Kim: we can do that. Task completion can trigger a workflow to update the company properties + append a note. BUT we need a standard set of outcomes.

Isabelle Laurent: ok, let’s define the dropdown values. My draft:
- Meeting booked
- No meeting / nurture (keep in marketing)
- Not a fit (disqualified)
- Existing customer / route to CSM
- Opp created
- Eval started

Ben Carter: can we collapse “opp created” vs “eval started”? we’ll get both sometimes.

Markus Klein: from SE side, “Eval started” is a real milestone (benchmarks, capacity sizing, security review). Often happens before a formal deal exists. I’d keep both.

Jasmine Liu: please keep “Existing customer / route to CSM” — we need to avoid double-touch. Also add a field for “CSM notified = yes/no” or make it automatic.

Tyler Benson: we can auto-notify CSM when outcome = existing customer. Don’t make reps fill another field.

Avery Johnson: what about “No response”? If I do Day 0/2/5 and nothing back, I need to close the task but it’s not disqualified.

Grace O'Connor: yes, SDRs need a “No response (cadence completed)” outcome.

Isabelle Laurent: revised list (final?):
1) Meeting booked
2) Opp created
3) Eval started
4) Connected / no next step (they replied but not ready)
5) No response (cadence completed)
6) Disqualified
7) Existing customer / handoff to CSM

Ben Carter: that works. For reporting, 1/2/3 are the main “wins”. 4/5 are “open / continue nurture”.

David Kim: cool. For #6 Disqualified, we should add `Disqual reason` dropdown, otherwise we can’t tune signals. Suggested reasons:
- False positive / low usage
- Budget
- Built in-house / competitor
- Security/compliance mismatch
- Timing
- Other

Stephanie Nguyen: make “False positive / low usage” explicit please. That’s the one we want to feed back into thresholds.

Markus Klein: + include “Needs Private / not ready” vs “Security mismatch”? Some are actually a fit but need Private, just too early.

Isabelle Laurent: maybe “Timing / not ready” covers that. If it’s “Needs Private” and they’re interested, that’s “Connected / no next step” not disqualified.

Jasmine Liu: agree. Don’t disqualify if it’s just “later this year”. That’s nurture.

Tyler Benson: implementation detail: If we capture “Connected / no next step”, marketing needs to suppress aggressive nurture for a bit (to avoid pile-on). Can we trigger a 14-day suppression on that?

Grace O'Connor: yes pls. Nothing worse than AE email + nurture email same day.

David Kim: I can add a workflow:
- When `plg_signal_outcome_latest` in (Meeting booked, Opp created, Eval started, Connected / no next step) => set `plg_outreach_suppression_until = now+14d`
- For No response => suppression 7d (optional)

Ben Carter: love it. Also need attribution back to specific signal. Are we storing a signal id?

David Kim: today we have `plg_signal_id_latest` and `plg_signal_type_latest` on Company + a timeline note with the full payload. When the follow-up task is created, we can stamp the `plg_signal_id` into the task subject like: `PLG Signal Follow-up (sig_01J...)`.

Avery Johnson: as long as I don’t have to copy/paste ids lol.

David Kim: you won’t. We’ll auto-populate. Workflow will read the task name and update company fields.

Isabelle Laurent: question: when a deal is created, how do we link it to the signal?

Tyler Benson: easiest: add deal property `Primary PLG Signal ID` and require it in deal creation template for pilot deals.

Stephanie Nguyen: requirement is fine for pilot, but keep it lightweight. Most AEs create deals fast.

David Kim: we can auto-set the deal property if the deal is created within X days of a signal and the company has `plg_signal_id_latest`. Not perfect but decent.

Ben Carter: can we do both? auto-fill, but allow override.

David Kim: yes.

HubSpot Workflow Bot: (system) Draft workflow created: “PLG Signal Follow-up Outcome Capture (PILOT)”. Status: disabled. Owner: David Kim.

Grace O'Connor: can someone post the exact instruction for reps? like 2 bullets.

Isabelle Laurent: draft enablement blurb for #sales:
- Close the “PLG Signal Follow-up” task after you finish the initial cadence (or earlier if they respond).
- Select Outcome (+ Disqual reason if applicable). That’s it.

Markus Klein: add: if Outcome = Eval started, loop in SE via @markus-klein / assign “SE Assist” task.

Stephanie Nguyen: yep. For Meeting booked + Private/Dedicated intent signals, please pull SE in immediately.

Jasmine Liu: and if Outcome = Existing customer / handoff to CSM, tag the CSM on the company record note (workflow can do it if we have CSM owner).

David Kim: workflow can create a task for CSM owner + add a note “PLG signal expansion detected”.

Ben Carter: perfect. Once this is live, I’ll update the dashboard to track:
- signals routed
- outcomes by type
- time-to-meeting
- disqual reason rates (esp false positive)

Isabelle Laurent: thanks all. David/Tyler can you implement dropdowns + workflow this week? We’ll announce in Friday standup.

David Kim: yep. I’ll post screenshots of the task field + the allowed values once it’s in sandbox.

Tyler Benson: I’ll handle suppression + attribution fields, and make sure marketing lists respect it.

Grace O'Connor: :thumbsup: once we have the dropdown, SDRs will comply.

Avery Johnson: :+1: please keep the outcome list short like we agreed.

Stephanie Nguyen: locking this. If anyone wants extra statuses, take it to ops :)

title: #sales: 1747246915
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: quick confirm for pilot v1 — when we get EU data residency signals, are we routing to EMEA ownership even if the org started self-serve in NA?

Lukas Meyer: yep, but with the “preserve existing owner” guardrail. If there’s already an AE owner in HubSpot (or active opp), we keep it and add me as follower / tag EMEA for assist. If no owner, route to EMEA queue.

Isabelle Laurent: +1. Rules we agreed in ops sync:
1) If `hubspot.company.owner_id` exists AND `lifecycle_stage` >= SQL OR `open_deals_count` > 0 → preserve owner.
2) Else if signal type == `DATA_RESIDENCY_EU` OR `SECURITY_COMPLIANCE_EU` → route to EMEA round-robin.
3) Else default routing by region (billing country / time zone heuristic).

David Kim: implementation detail: we’re setting `plg_routing_region = EMEA` when the signal is one of the EU residency intents. Workflow then assigns from the EMEA rotation list unless `owner_id` already set.

Ben Carter: what exactly counts as “EU residency intent” for v1? want to keep it tight so we don’t spam folks who just skim GDPR.

Lukas Meyer: propose we treat as residency intent only if they did one of:
- Viewed “Data residency / EU regions” docs page (not just privacy policy)
- Viewed Private/VPC deployment docs + EU region filter
- Submitted support form w/ “EU-only processing” or “in-country processing”
- Any message containing “data residency”, “EU region”, “Germany region”, “UK region”, “Schrems II” via intercom keyword tag

Grace O'Connor: for outreach, what do we send them? and do we mention Private immediately or start with Dedicated-in-EU?

Markus Klein: depends. If they’re explicitly asking “EU-only processing / customer data stays in EU” and they’re regulated → loop SE + point to Private/VPC options (and security review). If it’s “we need lower latency for EU users” → Dedicated in EU region is usually enough.

Stephanie Nguyen: ok. and what about accounts that *use* EU region but billing address is US (global SaaS)? do we still route to EMEA?

Isabelle Laurent: we said: ownership follows buying center, not traffic. So: if billing HQ is US + primary contacts are US → keep NA owner. But we should create a task template that flags “EU residency requirement” and suggests adding EMEA as assist (esp. if they need local DPAs).

David Kim: we can include a property `plg_signal_requires_emea_assist = true` on those. Workflow can @mention EMEA alias in the task note.

Lukas Meyer: + please make sure the task note includes what *exactly* triggered. “EU residency inquiry” w/o context is hard to triage.

Ben Carter: linking the draft playbook section here (WIP): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/PLG+Signals+Follow-up+Playbook#PLGSignalsFollow-upPlaybook-EMEADataResidency

Grace O'Connor: reading now. can we add a one-liner to the first email like “We can support EU-region deployments + discuss Dedicated vs Private depending on data boundaries”? want to avoid implying we’re automatically compliant.

Markus Klein: yes, and include a line: “Happy to walk through regions, logging/retention, and VPC options” + offer a 15-min security scoping call. Don’t promise “EU-only” without confirming which data types.

Stephanie Nguyen: cool. action items?

Isabelle Laurent: action items:
- David: confirm EMEA rotation list + workflow conditions for `DATA_RESIDENCY_EU` (preserve owner rule stays)
- Ben: tighten intent definition in playbook (exclude generic GDPR/privacy page)
- Lukas/Markus: add 3 discovery Qs + when to pull in SE (residency vs latency)

David Kim: ack. will update workflow + share screenshots in #sales-ops later today.

Lukas Meyer: thanks. once it’s in, please tag me on the first 5 EMEA-routed signals so we can sanity check.

Stephanie Nguyen: done. :thumbsup:

title: #sales: 1746987700
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: For the pilot, can we align on which PLG signals should *immediately* trigger SE involvement vs AE-only follow-up? I want this super explicit so we don’t spray Markus + team with every “pricing page view.”

Avery Johnson: +1. Last week I pulled Markus into a “high spend velocity” account and it ended up being a batch job that was already stabilized. Felt noisy.

Markus Klein: yep, pls gate it. IMO SE auto-engage should be reserved for (a) Private/VPC intent, (b) clear reliability/SLO pain, or (c) truly big throughput requirements where config/benchmarking matters.

Grace O'Connor: Can we define it as “SE required” vs “SE optional”? SDRs/AEs can still ask a couple questions before we page the SE.

Stephanie Nguyen: Works. Let’s draft the “SE required” list first.

Markus Klein: Proposal (SE REQUIRED):
1) **Private / VPC / on-prem intent**
   - docs events: `private-deployment`, `vpc`, `kms/hsm`, `audit-logs`, `data-retention` pages + any “contact sales” / demo request referencing VPC
   - or inbound message mentioning “VPC”, “on-prem”, “air-gapped”, “BAA”, “HIPAA”, “SOC2 evidence”, “DPA redlines”
2) **Reliability/SLO issues that look systemic**
   - repeated 429s/5xx over multiple days (not just one burst)
   - sustained p95 latency regression (e.g., >30% worse week-over-week) or consistent timeouts
   - customer explicitly says “we’re hitting limits / need higher concurrency / need predictable throughput”
3) **Large throughput targets / perf tuning**
   - “need X req/s” or “X tokens/sec” type ask
   - embeddings jobs with high QPS where batching/caching/throughput config matters
   - any account already at “Dedicated likely” thresholds + asking about benchmarking

Avery Johnson: Can we put some numbers so it’s deterministic? Like “429s on >=3 days in last 7” or “p95 latency above X for Y days”.

Isabelle Laurent: +1. If we make it objective, we can automate the HubSpot workflow without humans debating every time.

Markus Klein: Sure, but be careful: numbers vary by model + region. Still, we can set a baseline and treat it as “SE REQUIRED unless AE marks false positive.”

Ben Carter: From Growth side: we already have “reliability pain” signal class in the scoring draft. We can add a severity flag that maps to “SE required.”

Grace O'Connor: Also want to avoid SE auto-assign on pure “compliance curiosity.” Like viewing one security FAQ page shouldn’t page SE.

Jasmine Liu: Please include a CS guardrail: if it’s an existing paying customer w/ assigned CSM, looping SE should go through CSM first (or at least notify). We don’t want random outreach + SE scheduling over active support issues.

Stephanie Nguyen: Great call. Let’s add: “If company has Active CSM owner => notify CSM + AE, SE optional unless Private/VPC or severe SLO.”

Isabelle Laurent: Concrete rubric suggestion for pilot (NA):
- **SE REQUIRED** if ANY of:
  a) `intent_private_vpc = true` (>=2 qualifying doc views in 14d OR form fill mentioning VPC/Private)
  b) `reliability_sev = high` (>=3 days with 429 rate >=1% OR >=2 days with 5xx rate >=0.5% OR p95 latency > target by 30% for >=3 days)
  c) `scale_sev = high` (sustained spend velocity + explicit “need dedicated/throughput” intent)
- **SE OPTIONAL** if:
  - “Dedicated docs” + rising usage but no reliability/compliance pain yet
  - pricing page + docs but low usage (SDR qualifies first)

Avery Johnson: That’s good. For (c) can we define “explicit intent” as a doc view combo? Like dedicated docs + benchmark docs + “limits” help page?

Ben Carter: We can do “scale intent” as (dedicated docs views >=2 in 14d) OR (opened console limits page >=2) OR free-form text from contact sales.

Markus Klein: I like doc combos more than pure spend. Spend alone is sometimes just a scheduled backfill. I’d rather we require either (1) reliability pain or (2) intent signals that indicate they’re evaluating infra options.

Stephanie Nguyen: Agree. Spend velocity alone => AE task, not SE.

Grace O'Connor: So the rule is basically: “SE triggers only on (private/compliance) OR (reliability) OR (benchmark/throughput evaluation).”

Jasmine Liu: And if it’s triggered by reliability, please check if there’s an open support ticket first. If yes, route to Support/CS and let SE join if it’s clearly upgrade-related.

Isabelle Laurent: Operationally: we can add a HubSpot property like `plg_signal_requires_se = true/false` and `reason` enum (private_intent / reliability / throughput_eval). Workflow assigns Markus’ queue when true, but preserves existing SE owner if already set.

Markus Klein: Works. Also propose a capacity control: max 10 new “SE required” per week in pilot, otherwise overflow goes to “SE optional” review.

Stephanie Nguyen: I don’t love hard caps, but I get it. Maybe “if SE queue > N open tasks => auto-downgrade to optional + notify AE.”

Ben Carter: We can implement that as a suppression rule in routing service later. For pilot, we can do manual monitoring.

Avery Johnson: Can we get a quick “what I do before I pull SE” checklist? Like 3 discovery Qs.

Markus Klein: quick checklist (AE before SE intro):
- Are you blocked by **limits / concurrency / latency** right now, or planning for growth?
- Any **data residency / VPC / audit / retention** requirements?
- What’s your target throughput (req/s, tokens/sec) and what model(s)?
If any answer is “yes/unclear” on compliance or “yes” on limits w/ urgency => add SE.

Stephanie Nguyen: Perfect. Action items:
- Ben: update playbook section w/ SE REQUIRED vs OPTIONAL + checklist.
- Isabelle: translate into HubSpot workflow criteria (properties + assignment) and share draft.
- Markus: sanity check thresholds for reliability severity.

Grace O'Connor: :thumbsup: will also align SDR guidance so we don’t schedule “SE heavy” calls without context.

Jasmine Liu: + please add line: “If existing customer + CSM owner, tag CSM on task.”

Stephanie Nguyen: done. I’ll post the summary in #sales after Isabelle shares the workflow spec link.
text:
Stephanie Nguyen: For the pilot, can we align on which PLG signals should *immediately* trigger SE involvement vs AE-only follow-up? I want this super explicit so we don’t spray Markus + team with every “pricing page view.”

Avery Johnson: +1. Last week I pulled Markus into a “high spend velocity” account and it ended up being a batch job that was already stabilized. Felt noisy.

Markus Klein: yep, pls gate it. IMO SE auto-engage should be reserved for (a) Private/VPC intent, (b) clear reliability/SLO pain, or (c) truly big throughput requirements where config/benchmarking matters.

Grace O'Connor: Can we define it as “SE required” vs “SE optional”? SDRs/AEs can still ask a couple questions before we page the SE.

Stephanie Nguyen: Works. Let’s draft the “SE required” list first.

Markus Klein: Proposal (SE REQUIRED):
1) **Private / VPC / on-prem intent**
   - docs events: `private-deployment`, `vpc`, `kms/hsm`, `audit-logs`, `data-retention` pages + any “contact sales” / demo request referencing VPC
   - or inbound message mentioning “VPC”, “on-prem”, “air-gapped”, “BAA”, “HIPAA”, “SOC2 evidence”, “DPA redlines”
2) **Reliability/SLO issues that look systemic**
   - repeated 429s/5xx over multiple days (not just one burst)
   - sustained p95 latency regression (e.g., >30% worse week-over-week) or consistent timeouts
   - customer explicitly says “we’re hitting limits / need higher concurrency / need predictable throughput”
3) **Large throughput targets / perf tuning**
   - “need X req/s” or “X tokens/sec” type ask
   - embeddings jobs with high QPS where batching/caching/throughput config matters
   - any account already at “Dedicated likely” thresholds + asking about benchmarking

Avery Johnson: Can we put some numbers so it’s deterministic? Like “429s on >=3 days in last 7” or “p95 latency above X for Y days”.

Isabelle Laurent: +1. If we make it objective, we can automate the HubSpot workflow without humans debating every time.

Markus Klein: Sure, but be careful: numbers vary by model + region. Still, we can set a baseline and treat it as “SE REQUIRED unless AE marks false positive.”

Ben Carter: From Growth side: we already have “reliability pain” signal class in the scoring draft. We can add a severity flag that maps to “SE required.”

Grace O'Connor: Also want to avoid SE auto-assign on pure “compliance curiosity.” Like viewing one security FAQ page shouldn’t page SE.

Jasmine Liu: Please include a CS guardrail: if it’s an existing paying customer w/ assigned CSM, looping SE should go through CSM first (or at least notify). We don’t want random outreach + SE scheduling over active support issues.

Stephanie Nguyen: Great call. Let’s add: “If company has Active CSM owner => notify CSM + AE, SE optional unless Private/VPC or severe SLO.”

Isabelle Laurent: Concrete rubric suggestion for pilot (NA):
- **SE REQUIRED** if ANY of:
  a) `intent_private_vpc = true` (>=2 qualifying doc views in 14d OR form fill mentioning VPC/Private)
  b) `reliability_sev = high` (>=3 days with 429 rate >=1% OR >=2 days with 5xx rate >=0.5% OR p95 latency > target by 30% for >=3 days)
  c) `scale_sev = high` (sustained spend velocity + explicit “need dedicated/throughput” intent)
- **SE OPTIONAL** if:
  - “Dedicated docs” + rising usage but no reliability/compliance pain yet
  - pricing page + docs but low usage (SDR qualifies first)

Avery Johnson: That’s good. For (c) can we define “explicit intent” as a doc view combo? Like dedicated docs + benchmark docs + “limits” help page?

Ben Carter: We can do “scale intent” as (dedicated docs views >=2 in 14d) OR (opened console limits page >=2) OR free-form text from contact sales.

Markus Klein: I like doc combos more than pure spend. Spend alone is sometimes just a scheduled backfill. I’d rather we require either (1) reliability pain or (2) intent signals that indicate they’re evaluating infra options.

Stephanie Nguyen: Agree. Spend velocity alone => AE task, not SE.

Grace O'Connor: So the rule is basically: “SE triggers only on (private/compliance) OR (reliability) OR (benchmark/throughput evaluation).”

Jasmine Liu: And if it’s triggered by reliability, please check if there’s an open support ticket first. If yes, route to Support/CS and let SE join if it’s clearly upgrade-related.

Isabelle Laurent: Operationally: we can add a HubSpot property like `plg_signal_requires_se = true/false` and `reason` enum (private_intent / reliability / throughput_eval). Workflow assigns Markus’ queue when true, but preserves existing SE owner if already set.

Markus Klein: Works. Also propose a capacity control: max 10 new “SE required” per week in pilot, otherwise overflow goes to “SE optional” review.

Stephanie Nguyen: I don’t love hard caps, but I get it. Maybe “if SE queue > N open tasks => auto-downgrade to optional + notify AE.”

Ben Carter: We can implement that as a suppression rule in routing service later. For pilot, we can do manual monitoring.

Avery Johnson: Can we get a quick “what I do before I pull SE” checklist? Like 3 discovery Qs.

Markus Klein: quick checklist (AE before SE intro):
- Are you blocked by **limits / concurrency / latency** right now, or planning for growth?
- Any **data residency / VPC / audit / retention** requirements?
- What’s your target throughput (req/s, tokens/sec) and what model(s)?
If any answer is “yes/unclear” on compliance or “yes” on limits w/ urgency => add SE.

Stephanie Nguyen: Perfect. Action items:
- Ben: update playbook section w/ SE REQUIRED vs OPTIONAL + checklist.
- Isabelle: translate into HubSpot workflow criteria (properties + assignment) and share draft.
- Markus: sanity check thresholds for reliability severity.

Grace O'Connor: :thumbsup: will also align SDR guidance so we don’t schedule “SE heavy” calls without context.

Jasmine Liu: + please add line: “If existing customer + CSM owner, tag CSM on task.”

Stephanie Nguyen: done. I’ll post the summary in #sales after Isabelle shares the workflow spec link.

title: #sales: 1746731812
preview:
Avery Johnson: quick gut check on pilot credits/discounts. Northbridge is asking if we can “cover” the first month while they do security + benchmark. I don’t want to freestyle something and then get in trouble 😅 what are the guardrails for regulated pilot offers?

Stephanie Nguyen: +1, also coming up for Brightline. Can we get a crisp policy we can repeat in calls + follow-ups?

Ben Carter: following. we had the finance thread but need the "say it like this" version. @Laura Bennett can you confirm what you approved?

Laura Bennett: yep. CFO decision (for ABM pilot / regulated evals):
- We can offer *evaluation credits* (not “free month”) to support a time-boxed pilot.
- Default cap: **up to $15k in usage credits** for Hosted/Dedicated inference usage, **30 days**, expires in **60 days**.
- Credits apply to *usage only* (tokens/embeddings), not to platform fees, support add-ons, or on-prem/Private license.
- For **Private (VPC/on-prem)**: no waiver of platform fee as a default. If they need Private to evaluate, we do a paid pilot SOW or a short-term paid commit. Exceptions only with me + Stephanie sign-off.
- No stacking: can’t combine credits + additional discounts + marketplace incentives.
- Must have: named champion, target close date, agreed success criteria (MAP), and a scheduled security review. If procurement is >90 days out, we pause credits until there’s a committed evaluation plan.

Avery Johnson: super helpful. question: if they push for “we need $25k to make this work”, do we have any wiggle room?

Laura Bennett: case-by-case for *strategic* accounts only. Bring it to #deal-desk with (1) expected ACV, (2) pilot success criteria, (3) timeline. Not in-thread approvals.

Stephanie Nguyen: :thumbsup: can we also confirm wording re Dedicated? Some folks are asking for a % discount on the first term.

Laura Bennett: for Dedicated: no blanket discounting for pilots. If they want Dedicated capacity reserved to run the eval, that’s already real cost. We can structure it as:
- a **short initial term** + credits against usage, or
- a **reduced ramp** (smaller committed capacity month 1) with a planned step-up.
Any % discount needs sales leadership + finance approval.

Sofia Mendes: adding the legal/commercial note: please don’t put “discount” or “free” in writing unless it’s in an approved order form / SOW language. Call it “evaluation credits” or “pilot credits” and tie it to a defined pilot period.

Markus Klein: from SE side: can we align on the technical framing too? I’ve been saying “we can sponsor part of the benchmark cost so we can both validate SLOs quickly.” Works well if we keep it tight (30 days + clear pass/fail).

Ben Carter: +1. we should also avoid offering credits in first-touch outbound. keep it as a lever after they confirm the constraints + we know they’re real.

Grace O'Connor: yes please. SDR team will NOT mention credits in sequences. If asked directly, we can say “we sometimes sponsor a short evaluation for qualified projects” and then route to AE.

Avery Johnson: can someone give me a copy/paste snippet for prospects? Want to send to Northbridge after today’s call.

Stephanie Nguyen: draft below—Laura/Sofia please sanity check.

Stephanie Nguyen: 
“Happy to support a time-boxed evaluation. For qualified pilots, Redwood can include up to **$15k in evaluation credits** applied to inference usage during a **30-day pilot**, so your team can validate latency/throughput and complete the security review. We’ll align up front on success criteria and a target timeline for next steps; any usage above the credit amount would be billed per standard rates.”

Laura Bennett: looks good. Minor tweak: add that credits are not applicable to Private license/platform fees.

Sofia Mendes: agreed. Add one line: “Credits apply to inference usage only (not platform fees or support add-ons).”

Avery Johnson: perfect. sending that.

Isabelle Laurent: reminder for tracking: if you apply credits, log it in HubSpot under “Pilot credits approved” + amount + expiration date. Don’t bury it in notes only. I can add a property if needed.

Hannah Schmitt: for EMEA: same cap/terms? (we have one bank asking, but they want invoice in EUR)

Laura Bennett: same guardrails globally. We can invoice/credit in local currency but the cap is equivalent. Flag it in deal desk so finance doesn’t get surprised.

Ben Carter: thanks all. I’ll turn this into a 1-pager in the enablement folder + link in the ABM standup doc.

Markus Klein: :+1: and please loop SE early if Private is required for eval. we can usually find a VPC-based approach that doesn’t require on-prem day 1.

Avery Johnson: noted. thanks everyone.

(Internal note from Isabelle Laurent): :eyes: if anyone has already emailed “free month,” please forward to me so we can clean up language before it becomes a procurement anchor.
